title: elymus arenarius var. villosissimus
intro: Elymus arenarius var. villosissimus, also known as the sea couch grass, is a subspecies of the beach grass Elymus arenarius, which is a type of grass that grows in coastal areas and dunes. Traditionally, the roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to be beneficial in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Elymus arenarius var. villosissimus are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In terms of herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of this plant are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a powder to be consumed as a supplement.
